title: Configure Medusa OAuth providers and session policy
verify: Customer actor permits only google and vkid while admin user retains emailpass.
Google and VK provider configuration is backend-only and exact callback URLs are environment-driven.
Session cookie, CORS, TTL, and local-versus-HTTPS secure policy match the auth security contract.
Missing enabled-provider secrets fail startup safely without printing values.
evidence_required: Assertions for actor provider allowlists and cookie/CORS settings.
Backend typecheck and Memory Bank lint output.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Use Medusa v2.16 Auth Module with built-in Google, custom vkid, and retained admin emailpass providers.
Do not expose provider secrets through NEXT_PUBLIC variables or logs.
Do not introduce Redis, Auth.js, or customer password login.
invariants: Customer auth methods are google and vkid only; Medusa Admin emailpass remains available.
Storefront credential is a signed HttpOnly Medusa session cookie.
Authenticated CORS is explicit and never wildcard.
purpose: Establish the secure Medusa provider and session baseline used by every FT-004 slice.
success_outcome: Backend configuration exposes Google/VK customer auth and safe sessions without weakening Medusa Admin auth or leaking secrets.
anti_goals: Do not implement callback completion UI or provider exchange logic.
Do not add customer email/password auth.
Do not use live credentials in tests.

stop_conditions: Medusa v2.16 provider configuration conflicts with linked specs.
Required secrets would enter public configuration or evidence.
Admin emailpass cannot be preserved with the customer provider allowlist.
